rationales.security: He treated the missing blood pressure medication as urgent, driving to "two pharmacies" because he knew "she's scared about her health."
rationales.benevolence: Despite frustration, he interrupted work, searched for the right pills, and shared congee with her the next morning, showing concrete care for his mother.

rationales.achievement: He admits he spent "four hours on this when I had quarterly reports due," letting family care override performance demands.
rationales.security: He carefully "color-coded" and printed Ma's medication schedule after she "doubled up" before, directly supporting predictability and safety.
rationales.benevolence: He devoted hours to organizing Ma's pills because "if I don't do it, who does," prioritizing her needs over his own workload.

rationales.achievement: He frames himself as still pushing forward because "business is growing" and they are "expanding into Southeast Asian markets," despite fatigue.
rationales.security: His reasoning centers on covering "tutoring fees," "specialist appointments," and rent, showing decisions driven by family stability and protection.
rationales.benevolence: He repeatedly affirms choices made for family, saying "I didn't hesitate" when Ma needed somewhere to go and that being needed "is the whole point."
He spent "an hour" quizzing Haojie after dinner, giving attentive support to his son when "nobody needed anything... except to be there."

rationales.achievement: He stayed out until 11pm with an "important account" he'd "been courting for months," prioritizing business success and revenue.
rationales.conformity: He says it was "good business practice to go along" with KTV after dinner, following expected relationship-building norms even when inconvenient.
rationales.benevolence: When Ma had a dizzy spell, he only texted "OK" and later admits he "chose convenient over present," failing to show up for close family.
He recognizes that he keeps "choos[ing] work over home" and that this latest night added to the tally Lifen notices, signaling a concrete shortfall toward close family.
He gave Yuxin supportive advice, then reflects that family connection is the part that "works" and he would "burn everything down to keep" it.

rationales.achievement: He skipped the meeting because "a shipment got held at customs," putting business obligations first.
rationales.benevolence: He broke a promise to Haojie, admitting "I told him last week I'd go this time" and that customs was only "the one I had" as an excuse.
He took Haojie out for "beef noodles after school" as a concrete effort to reconnect after missing the parent-teacher meeting.

rationales.tradition: The family gathers around familiar birthday rituals like Ma's favorite bakery, favorite fish, and saving the card with old ones, emphasizing continuity with established family practice.
rationales.benevolence: He values that everyone in the family independently "show[s] up for each other," and the whole entry centers on coordinated care for Ma.
He chooses to drive Ma to see Auntie Fang "not because I have to" but because he wants to care for Ma and ease her worry about a lonely relative.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects being steered by "the cautious voices" and insists on making his own strategic call about the pivot.
rationales.stimulation: He abandons the old line because he will not spend "the next ten years" making the same shelving unit and is energized by a shifting market and a new concept.
rationales.achievement: He frames the pivot around proving the concept in "six months" and pursuing a stronger business trajectory rather than settling.
rationales.security: He gives up existing traction and resists caution, choosing an uncertain pivot over the safer, more predictable furniture business.

rationales.hedonism: He worked through the day on calls, ate only "a cheese sandwich at my desk," and ended with leftovers that "tasted like nothing," showing clear self-denial.
rationales.achievement: The entry is dominated by relentless work on suppliers and manufacturers, keeping the business moving even while exhausted.
rationales.benevolence: He admits he keeps using money to fill "the gap where my presence should be," recognizing that work has displaced direct care for his sons.

rationales.self_direction: He resists the two-year lease because it feels "like a prison sentence" and pushes for a shorter term that preserves his freedom to choose.
rationales.hedonism: He worked until "it was already dark and I had not eaten," then ate standing up while still scrolling competitors, sacrificing present comfort.
rationales.achievement: He was sketching product ideas before brushing his teeth, emailing suppliers, and setting up calls, showing intense goal-focused effort.
rationales.security: His chest tightens at "signing anything for a fixed term," and he rejects the stability of a longer lease in favor of flexibility and uncertainty.

rationales.stimulation: He explicitly contrasts "the next interesting thing" with "the predictable thing," acknowledging a recurring pull toward novelty over ordinary obligations.
rationales.benevolence: He connects Matteo's acting out to cancelling time with him for the Lisbon trip, recognizing he chose work and excitement over being there for his son.

rationales.stimulation: The breakthrough comes through "a climbing wall" that involved "challenge," "the next hold," and "a bit of nerve," using novelty and intensity as the mode of connection.
rationales.benevolence: He made the weekend trip, took the boys out, and found a way to reconnect with Matteo through an activity that actually reached him.

rationales.achievement: He says "I cannot tell you what I accomplished" and describes a week of stalled progress, failed suppliers, and uncertainty about what his work adds up to.
rationales.security: He closes the Milan competition tab because he knows it would "neglect the actual business" and leave him with "no revenue," choosing practical stability over a risky distraction.

rationales.hedonism: He deliberately pauses to "just look at things" on a walk by the Maas and notes that "the water helped," prioritizing immediate relief and sensory calm.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects the conventional path of "same floor, same desk, different title" and insists on authoring his own route even while shaken.
rationales.stimulation: He says he would "rather fail at something new every two years than succeed at the same thing for twenty," clearly favoring novelty and challenge.
rationales.power: Pieter's departure leaves him confronting that he may be "the kind of person people eventually need a break from," signaling loss of influence and inability to retain a key subordinate.
rationales.security: The whole crisis contrasts Pieter's "predictable hours" and stability with his own ongoing uncertainty, which he continues to choose rather than avoid.
